---
title: "Reproduce, Revise, and Reuse: practical examples"
---

This example demonstrates how to use AI to understand and review data.
it uses [Self-study Example 3](/selfstudy/example-3.qmd) as a starting point,
and expands on it by introducing **Plan mode** and features such as
**MCP**, **Tools**, and **Prompt files** as well as **skills**.

## Overview

This workflow incorporates best practices from:

- Claude's [Common workflows](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows)
  and [Best practices for code review](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/best-practices),
  which include:
- Day 1's [Common workflows](/methods/common-workflow.qmd).


## Step 1. Reproduce: Understand the code and data transformation to reproduce the analysis

```{.markdows file-name="Prompt"}
Audit this analysis code end‑to‑end. Produce a structured walkthrough
with sections: Inputs, Outputs, Step‑by‑step pipeline, Key
assumptions/decision points, Validation checks, Potential issues.
In the pipeline section, describe the data flow as “A → B → C”,
naming intermediate datasets/objects and the operations applied.
```

Run code:

Bad prompt

```{.markdown file-name="Prompt"}
Ran this code and produce the output. If it fails, identify the error and
fix it iteratively until it runs successfully. Then, produce the output.
```

## Step 2. Plan: review


```{.markdows file-name="Prompt"}
```
